async_execution.mechanism: hook_async_execution: async: true on command handlers runs hook in background without blocking; decisions ignored; systemMessage delivered on next turn

context_injection.mechanism: Hooks return systemMessage field in JSON output to inject context into agent's active session

decision_control.mechanism: block: exit code 2 or decision=block; allow: permissionDecision=allow in hookSpecificOutput; modify: updatedInput replaces tool input before execution

handler_types.mechanism: Four types: command (shell), http (POST to URL), prompt (LLM evaluation), agent (subagent with tools); prompt/agent restricted to subset of events

hook_scopes.mechanism: Six scopes: user (~/.claude/settings.json), project (.claude/settings.json), local (.claude/settings.local.json), managed policy, plugin (hooks/hooks.json), component frontmatter

input_modification.mechanism: hook_input_modification: PreToolUse returns updatedInput in hookSpecificOutput; entire input object replaced; compatible with all permissionDecision values except defer

json_io_protocol.mechanism: Command hooks receive event JSON on stdin; respond with JSON on stdout (exit 0); structured fields: continue, stopReason, suppressOutput, systemMessage, hookSpecificOutput

matcher_patterns.mechanism: hook_matcher_patterns: exact string, pipe-separated list, or JavaScript regex; matches on tool_name or event-specific fields

permission_control.mechanism: hook_permission_update_entries: PermissionRequest hooks return updatedPermissions with addRules/replaceRules/removeRules/setMode entries

providerExtensions.name: Hook Event Set
providerExtensions.summary: 24 hook events spanning session, turn, and tool-call cadences (SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PermissionRequest, FileChanged, etc.).

providerExtensions.name: Four Hook Handler Types
providerExtensions.summary: Hooks can be command (shell), http (POST), prompt (LLM eval), or agent (subagent); prompt/agent restricted to a subset of events.

providerExtensions.name: Hook Scopes and Locations
providerExtensions.summary: Six hook scopes: user, project, local, managed policy, plugin, and component frontmatter; managed policy can lock down the others.

providerExtensions.name: Matcher Pattern Syntax
providerExtensions.summary: Matcher field accepts exact strings, pipe-separated lists, or JavaScript regex; per-event fields (tool_name, source, reason, notification_type) drive matching.

providerExtensions.name: Decision Control via Exit Codes and JSON Output
providerExtensions.summary: Hooks decide via exit codes (0/2/other) or JSON fields (continue, stopReason, permissionDecision, decision, updatedInput, additionalContext).

providerExtensions.name: Async (Background) Hook Execution
providerExtensions.summary: Command hooks with async: true run in background; decisions ignored, systemMessage delivered on next turn.

providerExtensions.name: PreToolUse Input Modification and Defer
providerExtensions.summary: PreToolUse hooks return updatedInput to replace tool params before execution; defer (CC v2.1.89+) exits Claude for external resume via -p.

providerExtensions.name: Persistent Environment Variable Propagation via CLAUDE_ENV_FILE
providerExtensions.summary: SessionStart/CwdChanged/FileChanged hooks can write export statements to $CLAUDE_ENV_FILE that persist across subsequent Bash commands.

providerExtensions.name: Component-Scoped Hooks in Skills and Agent Frontmatter
providerExtensions.summary: Hooks defined in skill/subagent frontmatter run only while the component is active; skills also support a once: true handler flag.

providerExtensions.name: PermissionRequest Permission Update Entries
providerExtensions.summary: PermissionRequest hooks return updatedPermissions with addRules/replaceRules/removeRules/setMode/addDirectories entries scoped to session/local/project/user destinations.

providerExtensions.name: PostToolUse MCP Tool Output Override
providerExtensions.summary: PostToolUse hooks for MCP tools return updatedMCPToolOutput to filter/transform/annotate tool results before Claude sees them.

providerExtensions.name: WorktreeCreate Hook Replaces Default Git Behavior
providerExtensions.summary: WorktreeCreate hook fully replaces Claude Code's git worktree creation, returning the new worktree path; enables non-git VCS support.

providerExtensions.name: Elicitation and ElicitationResult Hooks
providerExtensions.summary: Elicitation/ElicitationResult hooks intercept MCP server input requests, returning accept/decline/cancel actions or modifying the response.
